# CPython configure check audit

This checklist compares each pinned CPython `pyconfig.h.in` symbol with `python/private/pyconfig.bzl` and generated `rules_cc_autoconf` manifests. The generator reads `configure.ac`; it does not execute `configure`.

The `configure.ac` columns contain lexical occurrences for source navigation; they are not producer classifications. Producer classifications come from the generated manifests and reviewed dispositions.

A checked row has a producer or an explicit reviewed disposition. An unchecked row is not audited and makes `--require-classified` fail.

Reviewed expected values are checked against every applicable generated configuration. Reviewed undefined symbols must remain undefined.

## Inputs

- CPython 3.11.15: `configure.ac` and `pyconfig.h.in` (596 symbols)
- CPython 3.12.13: `configure.ac` and `pyconfig.h.in` (613 symbols)
- CPython 3.13.13: `configure.ac` and `pyconfig.h.in` (642 symbols)

## Reviewed configure decisions outside pyconfig.h.in

These decisions are maintained explicitly because they do not emit `pyconfig.h.in` symbols.

| Decision | Classification | Implementation | Justification |
|---|---|---|---|
| `required_c99_math_functions` | `PROBE` | _required_math_checks() links all eight functions with -lm. | CPython treats acosh, asinh, atanh, erf, erfc, expm1, log1p, and log2 as required. |
| `dynamic_loading` | `PROBE` | _dynamic_loading_checks() checks dlopen with the platform link options. | HAVE_DYNAMIC_LOADING follows the actual dlopen result. |
| `posix_shared_memory` | `PROBE` | _posix_shmem_checks() links shm_open and shm_unlink with the platform link options. | Linux glibc 2.28 requires -lrt; Darwin provides the functions in libSystem. |
| `pty_functions` | `PROBE` | _pty_checks() links openpty, forkpty, and login_tty with the platform headers and libraries. | Linux uses libutil and Darwin uses libSystem. |
| `cpython_3_13_3_14_mimalloc` | `VERSION_SPECIFIC_BUILD_POLICY` | WITH_MIMALLOC follows HAVE_STD_ATOMIC, and Objects/mimalloc sources and headers are declared inputs. | This matches the CPython 3.13 and 3.14 default; CPython 3.11 and 3.12 do not use this policy. |
| `cpython_3_14_clang_22_ceval_stack` | `VERSION_SPECIFIC_COMPILER_POLICY` | python_ceval compiles Python/ceval.c with -finline-max-stacksize=512 on CPython 3.14 POSIX targets. | CPython 3.14 requires this option for Clang 22 computed-goto builds to prevent excessive _PyEval_EvalFrameDefault stack use. |
| `darwin_main_thread_stack` | `FIXED_TARGET_FACT` | THREAD_STACK_SIZE and -Wl,-stack_size,1000000 are set together in cpython.BUILD.bazel. | CPython requires a 16 MiB main-thread stack on Darwin arm64 and x86_64. |
| `darwin_corefoundation_link` | `FIXED_TARGET_FACT` | PLATFORM_LINKOPTS and LINKFORSHARED include -framework CoreFoundation. | This matches CPython's Darwin executable link contract. |
| `linux_system_libraries` | `FIXED_TARGET_FACT` | PLATFORM_LINKOPTS includes -ldl, -lm, -lpthread, -lrt, and -lutil. | The selected glibc 2.28 sysroot requires these libraries for the probed interfaces. |
| `zlib_dependency` | `DEPENDENCY_CONTRACT` | The zlib module depends on the pinned zlib Bazel module. | Header and function results are dependency contracts rather than host discovery. |
| `bzip2_dependency` | `DEPENDENCY_CONTRACT` | The _bz2 module depends on the pinned bzip2 Bazel module. | Header and function results are dependency contracts rather than host discovery. |
| `xz_dependency` | `DEPENDENCY_CONTRACT` | The _lzma module depends on the pinned xz Bazel module. | Header and function results are dependency contracts rather than host discovery. |
| `libffi_dependency` | `DEPENDENCY_CONTRACT` | The _ctypes module depends on the pinned hermetic libffi build. | ffi_prep_cif_var, ffi_prep_closure_loc, and ffi_closure_alloc follow that dependency. |
| `cpython_3_11_libffi_features` | `DEPENDENCY_CONTRACT` | cpython_static_module_manifest adds HAVE_FFI_CLOSURE_ALLOC, HAVE_FFI_PREP_CIF_VAR, and HAVE_FFI_PREP_CLOSURE_LOC only to the CPython 3.11 _ctypes target. | CPython 3.11 does not expose these extension-specific definitions through pyconfig.h.in. |
| `cpython_3_14_libffi_complex` | `DEPENDENCY_CONTRACT` | libffi_complex_test performs ffi_prep_cif and ffi_call with ffi_type_complex_double on every supported POSIX target. | The test reproduces CPython 3.14's runtime validation for _Py_FFI_SUPPORT_C_COMPLEX. |
| `openssl_dependency` | `DEPENDENCY_CONTRACT` | The _ssl and _hashlib modules depend on the pinned OpenSSL Bazel module. | OpenSSL configuration does not inspect host libraries. |
| `sqlite_dependency` | `DEPENDENCY_CONTRACT` | The _sqlite3 module depends on the pinned SQLite Bazel module. | PY_SQLITE_HAVE_SERIALIZE follows the pinned SQLite build; extension loading remains disabled. |
| `unsupported_optional_dependencies` | `UNSUPPORTED_DEPENDENCY` | readline, curses, panel, gdbm, ndbm, uuid, Tcl/Tk, editline, crypt, zstd, and external libintl symbols have explicit undefined dispositions. | The Bazel module does not declare those optional dependency graphs or modules. |
| `unsupported_build_modes` | `UNSUPPORTED_BUILD_MODE` | debug, shared-libpython, free-threading, statistics, trace-refs, DTrace, Valgrind, framework, universal, and perf-trampoline symbols have explicit undefined dispositions. | The version tag selects an ordinary static CPython build. |
| `hash_algorithm_default` | `HEADER_DERIVED` | Py_HASH_ALGORITHM remains undefined in pyconfig.h. | pyhash.h selects CPython's default when no configure option is supplied. |
| `pylong_digit_default` | `HEADER_DERIVED` | PYLONG_BITS_IN_DIGIT remains undefined in pyconfig.h. | pyport.h selects CPython's default when no configure option is supplied. |
| `sysconfig_generation` | `GENERATED_METADATA` | cpython_sysconfig reads generated pyconfig.h and an explicit Bazel build-variable manifest. | No Makefile execution or configure output is used. |
| `package_definitions` | `INTENTIONALLY_UNDEFINED` | checks.AC_FAIL emits explicit undefined results for all six PACKAGE_* symbols. | This overrides rules_cc_autoconf builtins and matches CPython's collision-avoidance rule. |
